_note: Zhang Fei murdered by Fan Jiang and Zhang Da (June 221); Liu Bei orders the eastern campaign against Wu in vengeance for Guan Yu.
Liu Bei rejects Zhuge Jin's peace mission; Sun Quan submits to Wei and is invested with the Nine Bestowments (Sept 221); the Shu army advances on Yiling.
Battles at Xiaoting (early 222). Text gives 'second year of Manifest Might' (Zhangwu 2 = 222, mis-stated as AD 221 in the translation). Huang Zhong dies in this campaign.
Lu Xun burns the linked Shu camps — climactic defeat at Yiling (June 222); Zhuge Liang's Eight-Array Maze covers Liu Bei's retreat to Baidicheng.
Liu Bei's death at Baidicheng (June 223) entrusting the Latter Ruler to Zhuge Liang; resists Wei's five-pronged response.
Sun Quan proclaims Huangwu era (October 222); Xu Sheng's fire repels Cao Pi at Guangling (224); Yong Kai's rebellion in Nanzhong forces Shu to plan the southern campaign (early 225).
Southern campaign launches — text explicitly cites 'third year of Beginning Prosperity (AD 225)'. First capture and release of Meng Huo (Spring 225).
Second and third captures of Meng Huo across the Lu River. Mid-225.
Fourth and fifth captures of Meng Huo, including the Tuhua tribesmen and the deer-poison stream. Summer 225.
Sixth and seventh capture of Meng Huo — wooden mastodons, Wuge Kingdom, the Silver Pit campaign. Final pacification of the south (autumn 225).
